subtasks.description: Extract licensed user counts and SKU details from SAM (Software Asset Management) system, including user type, license tier, and assignment status
Query consumption facts warehouse to retrieve active user counts, usage patterns, and spend data aggregated by SKU and user segment

Reconcile SAM licensed users against consumption active users, calculate utilization rates and variance, classify under-provisioned (high utilization, unlicensed users) vs over-provisioned (low utilization, excess licenses) segments, and generate spend optimization recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks provide minimal decomposition: (1) SAM extraction uses small tier—straightforward document/system field extraction of license assignments. (2) SQL query uses mid tier—requires joins across consumption facts, user dimensions, and SKU tables with aggregation by multiple grouping columns, but no complex window functions. (3) Cross-reconciliation uses frontier tier—must perform multi-signal analysis across two distinct sources (SAM state vs. SQL consumption facts), calculate variance metrics with ratio analysis, classify posture into discrete categories (under/over-provisioned), and generate nuanced spend optimization recommendations based on utilization thresholds and licensing rules. The frontier tier is necessary because the reconciliation requires inference logic to distinguish genuine under-provisioning (actual demand exceeds licensing) from temporary variance, and to recommend license adjustments with business context.

output.sql: SELECT product, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seats, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, AVG(amount_usd) AS avg_spend_per_event FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Microsoft' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY product ORDER BY total_spend_usd DESC
